etymology.description: 巴(巴) (bā, "to cling") + 掌(掌) (zhǎng, "palm") combine to describe the flat part of the hand, which can be used to deliver a slap. This imagery captures both the anatomical and functional aspect of the word.
etymology.interpretation: The term 巴掌(bāzhang) describes the palm of the hand, focusing on its use for clinging or grasping, and by extension, its ability to give a slap. This reflects a practical understanding of the hand's function.

examples.chinese: 他的巴掌很用力(他的巴掌很用力)。
小孩们在课堂上开玩笑(小孩們在課堂上開玩笑)，老师生气地拍了拍桌子(老師生氣地拍了拍桌子)，但没有打巴掌(但沒有打巴掌)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎohái men zài kètáng shàng kāiwánxiào, lǎoshī shēngqì de pāile pāi zhuōzi, dàn méiyǒu dǎ bāzhang.
examples.english: The children joked in class, the teacher angrily patted the table, but didn't slap.

examples.chinese: 他的脸上有一个红红的巴掌印(他的臉上有一個紅紅的巴掌印)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de liǎn shàng yǒu yīgè hónghóng de bāzhang yìn.
examples.english: There was a red palm print on his face.
